provcheck

Sign & verify C2PA Content Credentials

provcheck.ai · Creative Mayhem UG

Drop any file here to verify

Audio · Image · Video — anything that carries C2PA Content Credentials.

Or run with nothing and this stays put. Private — files never leave your machine.

Verifying…

—

—

Links

    Show raw manifest details

    Claims

    Signed outputs from rAIdio.bot and Doomscroll.fm verify here out of the box — try example: rAIdio.bot or example: Doomscroll.fm.
    Identity: not set up
    Session: disconnected

    Set up your signing identity

    This generates a cryptographic key on this machine and stores it in your OS keychain. You'll only need to do this once.

    The key never leaves your machine. Your OS handles access prompts.

    Connect your bsky identity

    Attach your signing key to your atproto handle so verifiers know it's yours.

    App passwords are scoped tokens — generate one at bsky.app → Settings → App Passwords. Never paste your account password here.

    Publish your signing key

    One last step — publishing your key's fingerprint to atproto lets verifiers cross-check that you signed your work. Without this they only see "valid signature by unknown."

    Or — works, but no atproto attestation.

    Your signing key is out of date

    This box's local key is marked retired on atproto. Verifiers won't attest signatures from it.

    Recover from a terminal:

    
          
    
            This mints a fresh key, publishes it as the new active record,
            and (when needed) revokes the orphan record so no one can sign
            in your name with the lost key.
          
    
        
    

        
    
          Or 
          — signature is valid but verifiers will see "signing key not currently attested."
        
    
      
    
    
    

    
    
    
      
      
    
        
    
          
    
            
            
    Drop a file here to sign
    
            
    Audio · Image · Video — anything c2pa-rs supports.
    
            
    Output goes next to the source as <name>.signed.<ext>.

    Your signature will join the chain as a derivative — the original signature stays intact.
